message:
test(ondisk): add four ext4 dir-block checksum sensitivity / detection relations (bd-anfg0)

The dir-block checksum proptest coverage in crates/ffs-ondisk/src/ext4.rs
was a single round-trip case (`ext4_proptest_dir_block_checksum_stamp_verify`)
that confirmed stamp-then-verify works.  Like the inode checksum harness
before bd-z75f6, that's a soundness check, not a behavioural-contract check
for the digest itself — it would still pass even if the per-inode seed
mixing were subtly broken or coverage stopped at the wrong byte.

Apply the same four-relation MR template the inode harness now uses:

- MR-INO (`..._is_sensitive_to_inode_number`): identical body, two
  different ino numbers ⇒ different stored checksum bytes; the second
  stamp must NOT verify when presented at the first ino (defends
  against cross-slot replay of a valid dir block).
- MR-GEN (`..._is_sensitive_to_generation`): same body+seed+ino, only
  the generation differs ⇒ stored checksum shifts.  Confirms the
  generation field really participates in the per-inode seed.
- MR-SEED (`..._is_sensitive_to_csum_seed`): same body+ino+gen, two
  different filesystem checksum seeds ⇒ different checksums; the seed-A
  stamp must not verify under seed B.  Defends against cross-filesystem
  stamp replay.
- MR-DETECT (`..._detects_single_bit_flip_in_body`): flipping any bit
  in the covered prefix `[0..bs - 12]` invalidates verification.  Pinned
  to the covered range via `prop_assume!(flip_pos < bs - 12)` so flips
  inside the 12-byte tail (which is excluded from coverage by both
  stamp and verify) are skipped rather than producing false negatives.

Also extracts a small `build_dir_block_for_checksum_test` helper so the
five proptest cases share the same well-formed prefix-ramp + fake-record
tail setup.

All five `ext4_proptest_dir_block_checksum_*` cases pass under
`cargo test -p ffs-ondisk`; clippy --tests -- -D warnings clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
